description: A monochrome engraving depicting a nude infant or putto seen from behind, standing and reaching upward to pull a cloth or garment over its head. The child's plump body is rendered with careful anatomical shading. The drapery cascades down from the raised arms. Cross-hatching provides tonal depth on the left side background. Text notations appear at the top and bottom of the print. The style is academic and classical.

_meta.title: Putto standing in place to clothe itself, by Guercino
